mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 2% signals that the central bank is maintaining accommodative policy, which compresses discount rates and supports equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative on average when FEDFUNDS < 2% over any 2-year rolling window, or the correlation between FEDFUNDS and forward returns is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months) signals that the economy is weakening sharply, leading to earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in a majority of instances where the trigger is activated over a 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth outpaces industrial production growth by more than 2% over 6 months, the imbalance signals inventory accumulation and future production cuts, which depresses corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: 6-month growth in RSAFS minus 6-month growth in INDPRO > 2%
failure_condition: In more than 60% of trigger events, forward 20-day returns are positive, or the spread measure has a zero or positive correlation with subsequent returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment (CPI YoY above 3%), further increases in consumer prices prompt the central bank to tighten policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In periods where CPI YoY exceeds 3%, increases in CPIAUCSL are followed by positive forward returns in more than 40% of cases, or the conditional correlation is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production of more than 0.5% signals an immediate supply shock that reduces corporate output and earnings prospects, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO declines by at least 0.5% month-on-month.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in housing starts is a leading indicator of residential investment and consumer spending, which boosts GDP growth and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative on average when HOUST is above its 12-month median, or the correlation between HOUST growth and forward returns is not significantly positive over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is low (below 2%) and retail sales growth is strong (above 5% year-over-year), the combination signals that the economy is overheating and the central bank will soon tighten, reducing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS < 2% and RSAFS YoY growth > 5%, forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of months, or the joint signal has no predictive power beyond single series.

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment regime (UNRATE below 4%), any further decline in the unemployment rate is unsustainable and signals an imminent recession as the labor market overheats, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE is below 4%, a decrease in UNRATE is followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the correlation between changes in UNRATE and forward returns is not significantly negative.
